name: Azure OpenAI Service API
what_it_does: Microsoft's hosted Azure deployment of OpenAI models (GPT-4, GPT-3.5, DALL-E, Whisper, Embeddings) with enterprise-grade compliance, private networking, and Azure AD authentication.
use_cases: Running GPT-4 in Azure with HIPAA/FedRAMP compliance for regulated industries
Enterprise LLM workloads requiring data residency in Azure regions
Embedding generation for RAG pipelines with Azure cognitive search integration
Azure AD-authenticated model inference for enterprise agent deployments
Whisper-based transcription with Azure reliability guarantees
not_for: Experimentation or prototyping (OpenAI API is simpler)
Teams without Azure subscriptions
Access to latest OpenAI models before Azure regional deployment
best_when: Enterprise teams needing OpenAI capabilities with Azure compliance, private networking, data residency, and Azure AD auth.
avoid_when: You need cutting-edge model access, flexible pricing, or simple prototyping - use OpenAI directly.

auth.notes: API key (Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key) or Azure AD token auth. Azure AD preferred for production. Resource-specific endpoints required.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAI-compatible error format; Azure adds deployment-specific errors
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Model inference with same inputs produces same schema (deterministic with temperature=0); completions are stateless
known_agent_gotchas: Azure endpoint URL differs from OpenAI - must configure SDK with Azure-specific base URL and API version
Model deployments must be created in Azure portal before use - unlike OpenAI's instant access
API version parameter required on every call - breaking changes tracked by version
Rate limits are per-deployment, not per-account - need multiple deployments for high throughput
Content filtering is enforced by default and may differ from OpenAI's defaults

security_score_components.security_notes: FedRAMP High, HIPAA BAA, PCI-DSS, SOC1/SOC2 compliant. Azure AD with RBAC provides fine-grained access control. Private endpoint support for VNet isolation. Content filtering adds additional safety layer.

name: Azure SDK for Python
what_it_does: Official Microsoft Python SDK providing client libraries for 200+ Azure services, enabling agents to manage Azure resources, consume Azure AI services, interact with storage, databases, and more.
use_cases: Managing Azure resources programmatically (VMs, storage, networking)
Consuming Azure AI services (OpenAI, Cognitive Services, ML)
Azure Blob Storage and Data Lake operations from agent workflows
Azure Key Vault secret management for secure credential handling
Azure Service Bus and Event Hub messaging integration
not_for: Non-Azure cloud infrastructure management
Teams preferring Terraform or ARM templates over Python SDK
Very simple Azure operations better handled by Azure CLI
best_when: You need programmatic Azure resource management or Azure service consumption from Python-based agent workflows.
avoid_when: You need multi-cloud abstractions, or your Azure use case is better served by Terraform/Bicep.

auth.notes: DefaultAzureCredential chains multiple auth methods. Managed Identity preferred in Azure. Service principal with client secret or cert for CI/CD.

free_tier_limits.note: SDK is free; costs depend on Azure services consumed

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Azure SDK exceptions are structured with error code, message, and request ID; helpful for debugging
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Long-running operations return pollers. Resource creation typically idempotent with same parameters.
known_agent_gotchas: DefaultAzureCredential tries many credential sources in order - silent fallbacks can cause agents to authenticate with wrong identity
Long-running operations (create VM, deploy) return pollers that must be awaited
Azure throttling returns 429 with Retry-After header - must respect these
Service-specific client versions must match across SDK packages to avoid version conflicts
Async clients require explicit close() or context manager usage

security_score_components.security_notes: Managed Identity eliminates stored credentials. RBAC with fine-grained roles. Key Vault integration for secret management. Azure AD token auth. FedRAMP, HIPAA compliant underlying services.

name: Backblaze B2
what_it_does: Low-cost cloud object storage with native B2 API and S3-compatible API, offering 1/4 the cost of AWS S3 for storing and retrieving files, with CDN integration options.
use_cases: Low-cost storage for agent-generated files, images, and data
Backup and archival storage for agent workflows
S3-compatible storage for teams migrating from AWS at lower cost
CDN origin storage via Cloudflare integration (free egress)
Large file storage for AI training datasets
not_for: Real-time latency-sensitive storage (S3 or GCS are faster)
Complex versioning or lifecycle policies at enterprise scale
Regulatory compliance requiring US-only FedRAMP storage
best_when: Cost is the primary concern and you need simple, reliable object storage with S3 compatibility.
avoid_when: You need sub-10ms latency, advanced S3 features, or strict compliance requirements beyond SOC2.

auth.notes: Application keys with bucket-level scoping. Master key for account-level operations. Separate keys per bucket recommended.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses with code and message; authentication errors are clear
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Upload by name is idempotent if using S3-compatible API. Native B2 API creates new file versions.
known_agent_gotchas: Two APIs (native B2 and S3-compatible) - pick one and stick with it
Native B2 API creates new file versions on upload - old versions accumulate cost
Authorization tokens expire - must reauthenticate periodically
Download URLs can be public or private with presigned URL generation
S3-compatible API requires endpoint override to B2's S3-compatible endpoint

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 certified. Bucket-scoped application keys. Server-side encryption optional. No FedRAMP or HIPAA. Simpler security profile than AWS S3 but adequate for most use cases.

name: BambooHR API
what_it_does: HRIS (Human Resource Information System) REST API for managing employee records, onboarding, time-off, performance, and HR workflows for small to mid-size businesses.
use_cases: Syncing employee directory data with other systems
Automating onboarding workflows by creating employee records
Retrieving time-off balances and requests for scheduling agents
Exporting HR reports for analytics pipelines
Managing employee document storage and signatures
not_for: Large enterprise HR (Workday or SAP are better suited)
Payroll processing (BambooHR has limited payroll - use ADP or Gusto)
Complex global HR with multi-country compliance requirements
best_when: SMBs need to integrate employee data into agent workflows or sync HR data with other business systems.
avoid_when: You need enterprise HRIS, global payroll, or complex labor compliance workflows.

auth.notes: API key via HTTP Basic Auth (key as username, 'x' as password). Company-specific subdomain required.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error messages vary in quality; some endpoints return plain text errors
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Employee creation and updates are not idempotent without checking existing records first
known_agent_gotchas: Some endpoints return XML by default - must set Accept: application/json header
Company-specific subdomain required in URL - not universal endpoint
API key has full account access - no scope granularity
Rate limits exist but are not clearly published
Custom fields vary per company - field names and IDs are company-specific

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. SOC2, GDPR, CCPA compliant. Single API key with full HR data access is a significant security risk. Basic Auth with API key is functional but outdated. No scope granularity for sensitive HR data.

name: Bandwidth
what_it_does: Tier-1 carrier and communications API providing SMS, MMS, voice calls, emergency services (E911), and phone number management with enterprise reliability and direct network access.
use_cases: Sending and receiving SMS/MMS in agent communication workflows
Making and managing voice calls programmatically
Provisioning and managing phone numbers for agents
Emergency calling (E911) integration for safety-critical applications
Building voice IVR systems with agent-driven call routing
not_for: Consumer messaging apps (Twilio has better developer experience)
Teams without enterprise messaging scale requirements
Simple notification-only SMS (SendGrid or Postmark with SMS add-on suffice)
best_when: Enterprise teams need carrier-grade reliability, direct E911 support, and own their phone number management at scale.
avoid_when: You need a quick, developer-friendly SMS API with minimal setup - use Twilio.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic Auth with username and password. Account ID and application ID required for many operations.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Structured error responses, but some endpoints return mixed XML/JSON
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Message IDs enable tracking but retries may create duplicate sends
known_agent_gotchas: Some Bandwidth APIs return XML, not JSON - agents must handle both or use SDK
Account ID, sub-account ID, and location ID hierarchy is complex
Phone number provisioning is separate from messaging configuration
Callback URLs required for receiving inbound messages - agents need public endpoints
E911 address validation required before emergency calling can be activated

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Basic Auth (username/password) is less secure than API key or OAuth. SOC2, HIPAA BAA available. No scope granularity - single credentials for all operations.

name: Bank API
what_it_does: Open banking API platform providing PSD2-compliant account information and payment initiation services for Dutch banks, with an MCP server for agent-driven banking integration.
use_cases: Checking bank account balances and transaction history via AI agent
Initiating payment orders programmatically under PSD2
Building financial overview dashboards with multi-bank data
Automated bookkeeping agents reading banking transactions
Reconciliation workflows comparing bank data with accounting systems
not_for: Non-Dutch/non-PSD2 banking environments
High-frequency trading or real-time market data
Business banking with complex multi-signatory requirements
best_when: You need PSD2-compliant access to Dutch bank accounts for reading transactions or initiating payments from agent workflows.
avoid_when: You need coverage outside Dutch banks, real-time trading, or non-PSD2 banking APIs.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with OpenID Connect. PSU (Payment Service User) consent required for account access. Scoped access per bank and permission type.

compliance: Dutch Public Sector REST API Design Rules

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard REST errors; OAuth consent flow errors can be unclear
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Payment initiation idempotency not clearly documented
known_agent_gotchas: OpenID Connect workflows may confuse agents without explicit OIDC handling
User consent (PSU authorization) required for each bank connection - cannot automate consent step
Bank-specific connection behavior varies - not all PSD2 features supported by all banks
MCP server is relatively new - documentation less mature than REST API docs

security_score_components.security_notes: PSD2-regulated and GDPR compliant. OAuth2 with OpenID Connect provides strong auth. Scoped access per bank and permission. User consent required - prevents unauthorized data access. OWASP API Security Top 10 2023 compliance documented.

name: Basecamp API
what_it_does: Project management platform API for managing projects, to-do lists, messages, documents, and team communication within Basecamp's opinionated collaboration structure.
use_cases: Creating and tracking to-do items from agent workflows
Posting messages and campfire updates programmatically
Syncing project status data with external dashboards
Automated project setup with standard Basecamp templates
Retrieving activity logs for project monitoring agents
not_for: Complex agile project management (Jira or Linear are better)
Real-time collaboration or chat (Slack is better)
Public project visibility or customer portal use cases
best_when: Teams already using Basecamp need to automate project workflows or integrate Basecamp data into agent systems.
avoid_when: You need complex agile workflows, real-time updates, or project management beyond Basecamp's intentionally limited feature set.

auth.notes: OAuth2 mandatory - no API key option. Access token required. Account-specific API endpoints per Basecamp account ID.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard HTTP errors; some operations return limited error detail
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: To-do and message creation is not idempotent - retries create duplicates
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth2 is mandatory - no API key option makes server-to-server automation more complex
Account ID required in all API URLs - differs per Basecamp account
Rate limit of 50 requests/10 seconds - clearly documented
Only Basecamp 3 API documented; Basecamp 2 has separate deprecated API
Recording objects (todos, messages) require knowing the parent bucket (project) ID

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 mandatory (good). GDPR compliant. Limited scope granularity - full account access once authorized. Rate limits documented.

name: Belvo API
what_it_does: Open finance API platform for Latin America providing account aggregation, transaction data, and income verification from 100+ LATAM banks for fintech and financial services agents.
use_cases: Aggregating bank account and transaction data across LATAM financial institutions
Income verification for credit decisioning agents
Building financial management apps with multi-bank data in Latin America
KYC/identity verification via bank account ownership confirmation
Automated transaction categorization for personal finance agents
not_for: North American or European banking data (use Plaid or Tink)
Real-time payment initiation (read-only in most markets)
Non-LATAM financial data aggregation
best_when: You need financial data aggregation from Latin American banks (Mexico, Brazil, Colombia) for fintech applications.
avoid_when: You need coverage outside Latin America, real-time payments, or US/EU banking data.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic Auth with Secret Key ID and Secret Key Password. Separate sandbox and production credentials.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Structured JSON errors with code and detail; institution-specific errors can be opaque
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Bank data retrieval creates new link/session each time; not idempotent
known_agent_gotchas: Bank data retrieval is inherently asynchronous and institution-dependent - timeouts are common
User credentials for each bank must be provided (or stored in Belvo) for data access
Institution availability varies - some banks have frequent downtime
MFA challenges require user interaction - cannot fully automate without user involvement
LATAM institution coverage varies by country - verify your target banks are supported

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. LGPD (Brazil) and LFPDPPP (Mexico) compliant. Basic Auth with key pair. Financial data requires careful handling - user bank credentials stored by Belvo raises trust requirements. No scope granularity.

name: Better Chatbot
what_it_does: An MCP server providing multi-model AI chatbot capabilities supporting 20+ LLM providers, with OAuth2 authentication, conversation management, and model switching capabilities.
use_cases: Multi-model AI orchestration through a unified MCP interface
Switching between LLM providers without changing agent tool definitions
Conversation history management across multiple AI sessions
Testing and comparing responses from different AI models
not_for: Production AI deployments requiring enterprise reliability
Stateless or context-window-constrained agent patterns
Organizations needing vetted security posture for AI tooling
best_when: You want to experiment with multiple LLM providers through a single MCP interface in a development environment.
avoid_when: You need production reliability, active maintenance, or vetted security for AI tool integrations.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authentication. API keys for each configured LLM provider must be separately configured.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Basic error messages; provider-specific errors may not be well surfaced
known_agent_gotchas: Development appears paused - security vulnerabilities may accumulate without active maintenance
Multiple LLM API keys must be configured - operational complexity increases with provider count
No documentation on security review or audit status
Chinese-language documentation predominant - may be challenging for non-Chinese speakers

security_score_components.security_notes: Development appears paused - security posture is unclear and potentially degrading. Stores multiple LLM provider API keys. No security audit documented. Chinese-authored, community-maintained with limited visibility.

name: Bifrost
what_it_does: MCP server and LLM proxy/router that provides a unified interface for multiple AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini) with load balancing, caching, and low-overhead routing.
use_cases: Routing LLM requests across multiple providers for cost optimization
Load balancing AI model requests across provider instances
Caching identical LLM requests to reduce cost and latency
Single MCP interface abstracting multiple AI backends
Provider failover when primary LLM is unavailable
not_for: Production-critical AI workloads requiring SLA guarantees
Teams needing vendor support or audit trails
Enterprise deployments requiring compliance certification
best_when: You need a lightweight LLM proxy to route between providers with caching in a development or internal tool context.
avoid_when: You need production reliability, compliance, or vendor support for your AI infrastructure.

auth.notes: Supports multiple auth mechanisms for connecting to downstream providers. API keys for each LLM provider configured separately.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Proxy passes through provider error messages; Bifrost-specific errors are minimal
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Caching provides some idempotency for identical requests; routing decisions are not idempotent
known_agent_gotchas: Performance claims (11µs overhead, 50x faster than LiteLLM) are vendor-stated and not independently verified
Multiple provider API keys stored in configuration - key management is critical
Community project with limited active maintenance signals
Load balancing strategy and failover behavior not thoroughly documented
Cache invalidation strategy unclear

security_score_components.security_notes: Stores multiple LLM provider API keys - significant security responsibility. No security audit documented. Community project with unclear dependency maintenance. Self-hosted means TLS is operator's responsibility.
